chapter_title.en: Chapter 31 – Satisfaction with the Judgement (of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/6)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/6)

parts.text: أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) أَنَّ

text: مَا أَوْحَى اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ إِلَى مُوسَى بْنِ عِمْرَانَ ( عليه السلام ) يَا مُوسَى بْنَ عِمْرَانَ مَا خَلَقْتُ خَلْقاً أَحَبَّ إِلَيَّ مِنْ عَبْدِيَ الْمُؤْمِنِ فَإِنِّي إِنَّمَا أَبْتَلِيهِ لِمَا هُوَ خَيْرٌ لَهُ وَ أُعَافِيهِ لِمَا هُوَ خَيْرٌ لَهُ وَ أَزْوِي عَنْهُ مَا هُوَ شَرٌّ لَهُ لِمَا هُوَ خَيْرٌ لَهُ وَ أَنَا أَعْلَمُ بِمَا يَصْلُحُ عَلَيْهِ عَبْدِي فَلْيَصْبِرْ عَلَى بَلَائِي وَ لْيَشْكُرْ نَعْمَائِي وَ لْيَرْضَ بِقَضَائِي أَكْتُبْهُ فِي الصِّدِّيقِينَ عِنْدِي إِذَا عَمِلَ بِرِضَائِي وَ أَطَاعَ أَمْرِي .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Isa, from Al Hassan Bin Mahboub, from Malik Bin Atiyya, from Dawood Bin Farqad,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>: ‘Among what Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic Revealed unto Musa Bin Imran<sup>as</sup> was: “O Musa<sup>as</sup> Bin Imran! I<sup>azwj</sup> have not Created a creature more Beloved to Me<sup>azwj</sup> than My<sup>azwj</sup> believing servant. So I<sup>azwj</sup>, rather, Test him for what is better for him, and Grant him health which is better for him, and Deter from him what is evil for him to what is better for him, and I<sup>azwj</sup> am more Knowing with what is correct upon My<sup>azwj</sup> servant. Therefore, let him be patient upon My<sup>azwj</sup> afflictions, and let him be grateful for My<sup>azwj</sup> Bounties, and let him be pleased with My<sup>azwj</sup> Judgement. I<sup>azwj</sup> shall Write for him to be among the truthful ones in My<sup>azwj</sup> Presence, when he acts by My<sup>azwj</sup> Pleasure and obeys My<sup>azwj</sup> Commands’.
en.sarwar: 7. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from al-Hassan ibn Mahbub from Malik ibn ‘Atiyah from Dawud ibn Farqad from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following:“Of the revelations that Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Holy, sent to Moses ibn ‘Imran was as follows: ‘O, Moses, son of ‘Imran, I have not created anything more beloved to Me than My believing servant. I try him only because it is for his good. I give him good health just for his good. I hold back what is evil for him in favor of what is for his good. I know best in what conditions My servant works for his own best interest. He then must bear patience in My trials, appreciate My favors and agree with My decision. I count him among the truthful ones before Me if he acts according to My pleasure and obeys My command.’”
